title: Coldenia fusca
taxonomy: Caldenia fusca is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is a member of the genus Caldenia within the order Lamiales. Caldenia fusca is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is a climbing vine that thrives in areas with high humidity and partial shade. Caldenia fusca is also known for its unique, yellowish-green flowers that attract various pollinators.

intro: Caldenia fusca, also known as Coldenia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae, native to tropical Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coldenia fusca include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Coldenia is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other plants to treat fever, cough, and sore throat.
